revision.comment: [[664年]]から[[685年]]まで日本で用いられた[[冠位]]。26階中11位。
revision.text:
'''小錦中'''（しょうきんちゅう）は、[[664年]]から[[685年]]まで日本で用いられた[[冠位]]である。26階中11位で上が[[小錦上]]、下が[[小錦下]]である。
== 概要 == [[天智天皇]]3年（[[664年]]）2月9日の冠位26階の制で、[[小花上]]と[[小花下]]の2階を[[小錦上]]、小錦中、[[小錦下]]の3階に改めて設けられた<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇3年（[[664年]]）2月9日条。以下、特に記さない限り『日本書紀』の該当年月条による。</ref>。[[大化]]3年（647年）の制度には[[小錦 (冠位)|小錦]]という冠位があって、大化5年（[[649年]]）に小花上と小花下に分割された経緯があり、小錦下などはその名を復活継承したものである。
[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685年]]）1月21日に冠位の命名方法が一新したときに廃止された。
== 叙位された人物 == 『日本書紀』に現れる小錦中の人物としては、[[天智天皇]]8年（[[669年]]）の[[遣唐使]][[河内鯨]]<ref>天智天皇8年（[[669年]]）是歳条。</ref>、[[天武天皇]]4年（[[675年]]）に広瀬河曲で大忌神を祭った[[間人大蓋]]、天武天皇10年（[[681年]]）3月17日に帝紀及び上古の諸事の記定を命じられた中の[[忌部子首]]、天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）に[[広瀬王]]とともに畿内で都にふさわしい地を探してまわった[[大伴安麻呂]]がいる。
天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）に死んだ[[星川麻呂]]と、同じく11年（[[682年]]）に死んだ[[膳摩漏]]は、没時に小錦中であったが、[[壬申の乱]]の功臣であったため、死後[[大紫]]位を贈られた。
『[[続日本紀]]』によれば、[[小野毛人]]<ref>『続日本紀』[[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]）4月15日条。</ref>と[[中納言]]の[[巨勢黒麻呂]]<ref>『続日本紀』[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]）6月6日条。</ref>も小錦中であった。しかし、小野毛人は江戸時代に発見された墓中の銘に[[大錦上]]とある<ref>京都国立博物館「[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html 金銅小野毛人墓誌]」、2009年6月閲覧。</ref>。
*[[河内鯨]] - 天智天皇8年（[[669年]]）見。遣唐使。 *[[間人大蓋]] - 天武天皇4年（[[675年]]）4月10日見。 *[[小野毛人]] - 天武天皇6年（[[677年]]）没。[[大錦上]]か。 *[[星川麻呂]] - 天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）5月27日没。[[大紫]]を贈る。 *[[忌部子首]] - 天武天皇10年（[[681年]]）3月17日見。 *[[膳摩漏]] - 天武天皇11年（[[682年]]）7月9日見。18日没。21日に[[大紫]]を贈る。 *[[大伴安麻呂]] - 天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）2月28日見。 *[[巨勢黒麻呂]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[冠位・官位制度の変遷]] [[Category:日本の位階|しようきんちゆう]]

'''小錦中'''（しょうきんちゅう）は、[[664年]]から[[685年]]まで日本で用いられた[[冠位]]である。26階中11位で上が[[小錦上]]、下が[[小錦下]]である。
== 概要 == [[天智天皇]]3年（[[664年]]）2月9日の冠位26階の制で、[[小花上]]と[[小花下]]の2階を[[小錦上]]、小錦中、[[小錦下]]の3階に改めて設けられた<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇3年（[[664年]]）2月9日条。以下、特に記さない限り『日本書紀』の該当年月条による。</ref>。[[大化]]3年（647年）の制度には[[小錦 (冠位)|小錦]]という冠位があって、大化5年（[[649年]]）に小花上と小花下に分割された経緯があり、小錦下などはその名を復活継承したものである。
[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685年]]）1月21日に冠位の命名方法が一新したときに廃止された。
== 叙位された人物 == 『日本書紀』に現れる小錦中の人物としては、[[天智天皇]]8年（[[669年]]）の[[遣唐使]][[河内鯨]]<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇8年（[[669年]]）是歳条。</ref>、[[天武天皇]]4年（[[675年]]）に広瀬河曲で大忌神を祭った[[間人大蓋]]、天武天皇10年（[[681年]]）3月17日に帝紀及び上古の諸事の記定を命じられた中の[[忌部子首]]、天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）に[[広瀬王]]とともに畿内で都にふさわしい地を探してまわった[[大伴安麻呂]]がいる。
天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）に死んだ[[星川麻呂]]と、同じく11年（[[682年]]）に死んだ[[膳摩漏]]は、没時に小錦中であったが、[[壬申の乱]]の功臣であったため、死後[[大紫]]位を贈られた。
『[[続日本紀]]』によれば、[[小野毛人]]<ref>『続日本紀』[[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]）4月15日条。</ref>と[[中納言]]の[[巨勢黒麻呂]]<ref>『続日本紀』[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]）6月6日条。</ref>も小錦中であった。しかし、小野毛人は江戸時代に発見された墓中の銘に[[大錦上]]とある<ref>京都国立博物館「[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html 金銅小野毛人墓誌]」、2009年6月閲覧。</ref>。
*[[河内鯨]] - 天智天皇8年（[[669年]]）見。遣唐使。 *[[間人大蓋]] - 天武天皇4年（[[675年]]）4月10日見。 *[[小野毛人]] - 天武天皇6年（[[677年]]）没。[[大錦上]]か。 *[[星川麻呂]] - 天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）5月27日没。[[大紫]]を贈る。 *[[忌部子首]] - 天武天皇10年（[[681年]]）3月17日見。 *[[膳摩漏]] - 天武天皇11年（[[682年]]）7月9日見。18日没。21日に[[大紫]]を贈る。 *[[大伴安麻呂]] - 天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）2月28日見。 *[[巨勢黒麻呂]] - 中納言。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[冠位・官位制度の変遷]] [[Category:日本の位階|しようきんちゆう]]

[[ja:小錦中]]

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[冠位|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

revision.text:
[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[冠位|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shōkinchū}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:New pages]][[Cagetory:Japanese court rank system]]

== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

[[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[冠位|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷}}

== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[冠位|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[冠位|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[冠位|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[冠位|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[冠位|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court ranks in Japan|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court ranks in Japan|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.

Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court ranks in Japan|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}}

Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court ranks in Japan|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}}

Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}}

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court ranks in Japan|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>

revision.text:
[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court ranks in Japan|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}}

[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court ranks in Japan|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}}

== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=draft}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=draft}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=draft}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=draft}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=draft}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=draft}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=draft}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=draft}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court ranks in Japan|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上|12=draft}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階|12=draft}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下|12=draft}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新|12=draft}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦|12=draft}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=draft}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=draft}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=draft}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=draft}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=draft}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=draft}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=draft}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=draft}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=draft}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=draft}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=draft}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=draft}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=draft}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=draft}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=draft}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=draft}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=draft}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''小錦中'''（しょうきんちゅう）は、[[664年]]から[[685年]]まで日本で用いられた[[冠位]]である。26階中11位で上が[[小錦上]]、下が[[小錦下]]である。
== 概要 == [[天智天皇]]3年（[[664年]]）2月9日の冠位26階の制で、[[小花上]]と[[小花下]]の2階を[[小錦上]]、小錦中、[[小錦下]]の3階に改めて設けられた<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇3年（[[664年]]）2月9日条。以下、特に記さない限り『日本書紀』の該当年月条による。</ref>。[[大化]]3年（647年）の制度には[[小錦 (冠位)|小錦]]という冠位があって、大化5年（[[649年]]）に小花上と小花下に分割された経緯があり、小錦下などはその名を復活継承したものである。
[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685年]]）1月21日に冠位の命名方法が一新したときに廃止された。
== 叙位された人物 == 『日本書紀』に現れる小錦中の人物としては、[[天智天皇]]8年（[[669年]]）の[[遣唐使]][[河内鯨]]<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇8年（[[669年]]）是歳条。</ref>、[[天武天皇]]4年（[[675年]]）に広瀬河曲で大忌神を祭った[[間人大蓋]]、天武天皇10年（[[681年]]）3月17日に帝紀及び上古の諸事の記定を命じられた中の[[忌部子首]]、天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）に[[広瀬王]]とともに畿内で都にふさわしい地を探してまわった[[大伴安麻呂]]がいる。
天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）に死んだ[[星川麻呂]]と、同じく11年（[[682年]]）に死んだ[[膳摩漏]]は、没時に小錦中であったが、[[壬申の乱]]の功臣であったため、死後[[大紫]]位を贈られた。
『[[続日本紀]]』によれば、[[小野毛人]]<ref>『続日本紀』[[和銅]]7年（[[714年]]）4月15日条。</ref>と[[中納言]]の[[巨勢黒麻呂]]<ref>『続日本紀』[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]）6月6日条。</ref>も小錦中であった。しかし、小野毛人は江戸時代に発見された墓中の銘に[[大錦上]]とある<ref>京都国立博物館「[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html 金銅小野毛人墓誌]」、2009年6月閲覧。</ref>。
*[[河内鯨]] - 天智天皇8年（[[669年]]）見。遣唐使。 *[[間人大蓋]] - 天武天皇4年（[[675年]]）4月10日見。 *[[小野毛人]] - 天武天皇6年（[[677年]]）没。[[大錦上]]か。 *[[星川麻呂]] - 天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）5月27日没。[[大紫]]を贈る。 *[[忌部子首]] - 天武天皇10年（[[681年]]）3月17日見。 *[[膳摩漏]] - 天武天皇11年（[[682年]]）7月9日見。18日没。21日に[[大紫]]を贈る。 *[[大伴安麻呂]] - 天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）2月28日見。 *[[巨勢黒麻呂]] - 中納言。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[冠位・位階制度の変遷]]
{{デフォルトソート:しようきんちゆう}} [[Category:冠位制度]]

*[[河内鯨]] - 天智天皇8年（[[669年]]）見。遣唐使。 *[[間人大蓋]] - 天武天皇4年（[[675年]]）4月10日見。 *[[小野毛人]] - 天武天皇6年（[[677年]]）没。[[大錦上]]か。 *[[星川麻呂]] - 天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）5月27日没。[[大紫]]を贈る。 *[[忌部子首]] - 天武天皇10年（[[681年]]）3月17日見。 *[[膳摩漏]] - 天武天皇11年（[[682年]]）7月9日見。18日没。21日に[[大紫]]を贈る。 *[[大伴安麻呂]] - 天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）2月28日見。 *[[巨勢黒麻呂]] - 中納言。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[冠位・位階制度の変遷]]

== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the Rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
