revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11464366]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court Ranks in Japan|court Rank]] used in Japan from [[:en:664|664]] to [[:en:685|685]]. It was the 11th Rank in a 26-Rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinjō|1=Shōkin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464365}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinge|1=Shōkin-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464364}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-Rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26-Rank cap system|qq=}}. It replaced the two Ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Jō|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Ge|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}, which were reorganized into the three Ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}} of 647, a Rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkin|1=Shōkin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464362}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related Ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This Rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for Ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the Rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and position changes in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

It replaced the two Ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Jō|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Ge|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}, which were reorganized into the three Ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}} of 647, a Rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkin|1=Shōkin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464362}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related Ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This Rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for Ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the Rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and position changes in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

<!--Shōkinchū-->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11464366}} [[Category:Cap-and-Rank System]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:qq]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11464366]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court Ranks in Japan|court Rank]] used in Japan from [[:en:664|664]] to [[:en:685|685]]. It was the 11th Rank in a 26-Rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinjō|1=Shōkin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464365}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinge|1=Shōkin-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464364}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-Rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26-Rank cap system|qq=}}. It replaced the two Ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Jō|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Ge|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}, which were reorganized into the three Ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}} of 647, a Rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkin|1=Shōkin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464362}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related Ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This Rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for Ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the Rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

Shōkinge and related Ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This Rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for Ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the Rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and position changes in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11464366]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court Ranks in Japan|court Rank]] used in Japan from {{ill|664|en|664|WD=Q30621|lt=664}} to {{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685}}. It was the 11th Rank in a 26-Rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinjō|1=Shōkin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464365}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinge|1=Shōkin-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464364}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-Rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26-Rank cap system|qq=}}. It replaced the two Ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Jō|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Ge|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}, which were reorganized into the three Ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}} of 647, a Rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkin|1=Shōkin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464362}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related Ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This Rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for Ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the Rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and position changes in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11464366]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court Ranks in Japan|court Rank]] used in Japan from {{ill|664|en|664|WD=Q30621|lt=664}} to {{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685}}. It was the 11th Rank in a 26-Rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinjō|1=Shōkin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464365}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinge|1=Shōkin-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464364}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-Rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26-Rank cap system|qq=}}. It replaced the two Ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Jō|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Ge|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}, which were reorganized into the three Ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}} of 647, a Rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkin|1=Shōkin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464362}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related Ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This Rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for Ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the Rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and position changes in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

It was the 11th Rank in a 26-Rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinjō|1=Shōkin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464365}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinge|1=Shōkin-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464364}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-Rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26-Rank cap system|qq=}}. Shōkinge and related Ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This Rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for Ranks were completely revised.

Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>

Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - == Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the Rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Hoshikawa no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Inabe no Shikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Kashiwade no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Rank and position changes in Japan|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank and position changes in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]]
'''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court Ranks in Japan|court Rank]] used in Japan from {{ill|664|en|664|WD=Q30621|lt=664|1=664}} to {{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685|1=685}}. It was the 11th Rank in a 26-Rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinjō|1=Shōkin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464365}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinge|1=Shōkin-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464364}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-Rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26-Rank cap system|qq=}}. It replaced the two Ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Jō|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Ge|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}, which were reorganized into the three Ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}} of 647, a Rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkin|1=Q11464362|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464362}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related Ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This Rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for Ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the Rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Q11490720|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Q11512907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Q11612411|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Q11512907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Q11490720|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Q11612411|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小錦中]]
'''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[Court Ranks in Japan|court Rank]] used in Japan from {{ill|664|en|664|WD=Q30621|lt=664|1=664}} to {{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685|1=685}}. It was the 11th Rank in a 26-Rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinjō|1=Shōkin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464365}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinge|1=Shōkin-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464364}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-Rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26-Rank cap system|qq=}}. It replaced the two Ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Jō|1=Shōhana-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463510}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobana Ge|1=Shōhana-ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463512}}, which were reorganized into the three Ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}} of 647, a Rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkin|1=Q11464362|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464362}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related Ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This Rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for Ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the Rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Q11490720|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the Rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Q11512907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Q11612411|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Possibly held the Rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kujira|1=Kawachi no Kujira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59134795}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maishin no Oogai|1=Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Azumabito|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual Rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikinjō|1=Daikin-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439797}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Maro|1=Q11512907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512907}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Shikō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 忌部子人|1=Q11490720|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490720}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Maro|1=Q11612411|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612411}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438476}}. * {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kuromaro|ja|巨勢黒麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kuromaro|qq=}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
